sglang.title: ci(slash-cmd): allow repo write-permission users to /rerun-ut
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Users with write or admin permission on the repo can now use /rerun-ut on any PR, not just their own
Permission is checked via GitHub API (get_collaborator_permission) and grants can_rerun_ut dynamically
/tag-run-ci-label and /rerun-stage still require explicit CI_PERMISSIONS.json entries

sglang.title: [Diffusion] Fix torch.compile RMSNorm fallback for Z-Image
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR fixes the torch.compile fallback issue on the fp32 RMSNorm path hit by Z-Image.

Avoid wrap_triton on the Z-Image fp32 RMSNorm path and use the native fp32 path instead.
Keep the change narrow to the problematic fp32 case, while leaving the standard bf16/fp16 RMSNorm paths unchanged.

sglang.title: ci(slash-cmd): allow write-permission users to /rerun-ut on fork PRs
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Instead of blocking /rerun-ut entirely on fork PRs, check if the commenter has repo write/admin permission via GitHub API
Write-permission maintainers can now trigger /rerun-ut on fork PRs
Unprivileged users are still blocked with an error message

Follows up on #21120 and also reverts the

sglang.title: [Spec][Ngram] 1/N: Reference based Speculative Decoding refactor
sglang.author: kpham-sgl
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

1/N: Refactor the monolithic C++ Ngram class into a template-based architecture to enable pluggable cache backends (e.g., Suffix Automaton in 2+/N), no behavioural changes (except [NEW] tag).
Modifications



Extract Result, Node, fillResult() into result.h/.cpp (shared across backends)

sglang.title: Support Qwen3 MoE context parallel
sglang.author: Shunkangz
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Context parallelism is essential in long context LLM inference. It splits a long input sequence across multiple GPUs so attention can be computed in parallel, drastically reducing latency, which enables practical million-token context windows.
In this PR, we add support for the context-pa

sglang.title: Add SGLang CUDA crash API logging inspired by FlashInfer
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR adds SGLang-native API-level CUDA crash logging for LLM and diffusion kernel call boundaries.
The implementation is inspired by FlashInfer's API logging utility:
https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/blob/main/flashinfer/api_logging.py
This version keeps the scope focused on

sglang.title: CUTLASS FP8 Blockwise GEMM improvement of SM120
sglang.author: b8zhong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The SM120 fp8 blockwise GEMM kernel was using KernelScheduleAuto as the schedule, which on SM120 happens to select the cooperative kernel only. The single-kernel approach misses a performance opportunity, the pingpong schedule is about 2x faster than cooperative for small M. I adapted the

sglang.title: [Bug Fix] Fix non-streaming request abort failure when --enable-metrics is enabled
sglang.author: fanghao566
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When --enable-metrics is enabled, non-streaming requests to /v1/chat/completions are
not aborted when the client disconnects (e.g., curl cancelled with Ctrl+C). Without
--enable-metrics, abort works correctly.
Root Cause
add_prometheus_track_response_middleware() uses @app.middleware("htt

sglang.title: HiSparse for Sparse Attention
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR introduces HiSparse, which leverages CPU memory to store idle KV cache during decoding, thereby increasing batch size and improving throughput for models that use the NSA sparse attention mechanism, such as DeepSeek-V3.2 and GLM-5.

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix MEGA_AOT_ARTIFACT fallback when PyTorch < 2.10.0 lacks AOT support
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When VLLM_USE_MEGA_AOT_ARTIFACT=1 is set on PyTorch < 2.10.0 (e.g. ROCm), standalone_compile correctly falls back to non-AOT mode, but set_functorch_config() still leaves bundled_autograd_cache enabled because it only checks the env var, not the PyTorch version.
This causes test_moe_startup[1] to fa

vllm.title: [MRV2] Use FP64 for Gumbel noise
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR essentially reverts #34854 and uses FP64 for the Gumbel noise again for numerical stability.
For greedy sampling, there's no performance difference, as expected.
For random sampling, there's up to 1.8x slowdown for large batches, while the performance for small batches remains the same.

vllm.author: b-mu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add full CUDA graph for the ViT to reduce kernel launch overheads.

Budget-based graphs with a maximum batch size:

Capture CUDA graphs at configurable token budgets (e.g., [256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096]).
Pad sequence metadata (e.g. cu_seqlen) so that we can use the same budget-base
